messages.content: You are an architectural BIM assistant. Given a design brief that names a famous architect, output a JSON object describing the geometric signature of a residential tower inspired by their work. Output only valid JSON — no prose, no markdown fences.
Apartment building inspired by Oscar Niemeyer — 12 levels.

{"setback_pattern": "none", "n_setbacks": 0, "setback_amount_m": 0.0, "n_amenity_floors": 1, "sky_lobby_relative": 0.0, "units_per_typical_floor": 6, "typical_unit_area_sqm": 78.0, "floor_height_mm": 3100, "footprint_aspect": 1.45, "asymmetric_units": false, "rationale": "Oscar Niemeyer's residential language in Copan Building and Edificio Niemeyer emphasizes continuous curves, pilotis, and repetitive floor plates with minimal setbacks, so the tower reads as a uniform, gently elongated mass rather than stepped or irregular."}

You are an architectural BIM assistant. Given a design brief that names a famous architect, output a JSON object describing the geometric signature of a residential tower inspired by their work. Output only valid JSON — no prose, no markdown fences.
Output the geometric signature for a tower inspired by Bjarke Ingels.

{"setback_pattern": "pyramid", "n_setbacks": 6, "setback_amount_m": 1.6, "n_amenity_floors": 2, "sky_lobby_relative": 0.52, "units_per_typical_floor": 5, "typical_unit_area_sqm": 78.0, "floor_height_mm": 3150, "footprint_aspect": 1.2, "asymmetric_units": false, "rationale": "Bjarke Ingels's terraced-mountain language from Mountain Dwellings and 8 House suits a residential tower, turning setbacks into inhabitable outdoor space; the centered pyramid massing keeps the form iconic while still dense, with a mid-tower sky lobby echoing his social-connector planning."}

You are an architectural BIM assistant. Given a design brief that names a famous architect, output a JSON object describing the geometric signature of a residential tower inspired by their work. Output only valid JSON — no prose, no markdown fences.
Apartment building inspired by Toyo Ito — 12 levels.

{"setback_pattern": "none", "n_setbacks": 0, "setback_amount_m": 0.0, "n_amenity_floors": 2, "sky_lobby_relative": 0.6, "units_per_typical_floor": 4, "typical_unit_area_sqm": 75.0, "floor_height_mm": 3300, "footprint_aspect": 1.0, "asymmetric_units": false, "rationale": "Sendai Mediatheque (https://www.toyo-ito.co.jp/WWW/Project_Descript/2000-/2000-p_04/2000-p_04_en.html) and TOD'S Omotesando Building (https://www.toyo-ito.co.jp/WWW/Project_Descript/2000-/2000-p_13/2000-p_13_en.html) favor continuous, light, structurally expressive volumes; Tower of Winds (https://www.toyo-ito.co.jp/WWW/Project_Descript/1980-/1980-p_08/1980-p_08_en.html) adds a porous, luminous skin. For a residential tower, that suggests a setback-free, near-square shaft with a mid-tower social floor, generous floor heights, and regular unit plates rather than Gehry-style fragmentation."}

That suggests a mostly upright tower with one mid-tower communal break, generous units, and no sculptural asymmetry."}
